teams.notes: Pinned to the published playoff-pain-index site numbers (canonical). NOTE: independent research found 132 (Semis=5, not 7) with primary-source support; pinned value follows the live site.

flags: 2020 season cancelled due to COVID-19; excluded from drought window counts
2014 and 2018 Division Semifinal losses were crossover playoff appearances (played in Eastern bracket); counted as Division Semifinal losses
2017, 2019, 2021 missed playoffs with losing records; no winningRecordNoPlayoff credited
teams.notes: BC Lions won the 2011 Grey Cup over Winnipeg 34-23. Drought window is 2012-2025. All missed-playoff seasons had losing records so no winningRecordNoPlayoff applies. PPI score = 8*5 + 4*5 = 40 + 20 = 60.

teams.lastChampionship: 2021 Grey Cup (108th, def. Hamilton Tiger-Cats 33-25 OT)
flags: 2025: Winnipeg crossed over from West to East playoffs under CFL crossover rule (4th West, better record than 3rd East); lost East Division Semi-Final to Montreal 42-33 -- counted as Quarters (Division Semi-Final) loss
2022 Grey Cup loss: Toronto Argonauts 24-23
2023 Grey Cup loss: Montreal Alouettes 28-24
2024 Grey Cup loss: Toronto Argonauts 41-24
teams.notes: Winnipeg won back-to-back Grey Cups in 2019 and 2021 (no 2020 season due to COVID). Since the 2021 title they have reached three straight Grey Cup Finals (2022, 2023, 2024), losing all three. In 2025 they slipped to 4th in the West (10-8), crossed over into the East bracket under the CFL crossover rule, and lost the East Division Semi-Final to Montreal. Score = 16*3 + 4*1 = 52.

flags: 2020 CFL season was cancelled due to COVID-19; not counted
2016: Crossover to East; won East Semi-Final vs Hamilton, lost East Final vs Ottawa (Division Final = semis)
2017: Lost West Final vs Calgary (Division Final = semis)
2018: 9-9 record, missed playoffs; not a clearly winning record, not counted
2019: Crossover to East; won East Semi-Final vs Montreal, lost East Final vs Hamilton (Division Final = semis)
2021-2025: Missed playoffs with losing records (3-11, 4-14, 4-14, 7-11, 7-11); not counted
teams.notes: Edmonton last won the Grey Cup in 2015. The drought window covers 2016-2025 (10 completed seasons; 2020 cancelled). In that span they reached the Division Final (one step from Grey Cup) three times: 2016, 2017, 2019 — each counts as a semi-final loss. They missed the playoffs entirely 2018, 2021-2025, but none of those seasons featured a clearly winning record.

Drought window is only 2 completed seasons (2024-2025). 2024: lost to Toronto Argonauts in East Final (Division Final, one round before the Grey Cup = semis). 2025: lost to Saskatchewan Roughriders 25-17 in the 112th Grey Cup = finals loss.

flags: 2020 CFL season was cancelled due to COVID-19 and is not counted
2017 East Semi-Final loss confirmed via ottawaredblacks.com article headline
2018 Grey Cup loss to Calgary confirmed (27-16)
2024 East Semi-Final loss to Toronto confirmed (58-38)
2020 season cancelled due to COVID-19 pandemic — not counted
2023 was first losing-record season (6-12) since 2007 but Stampeders still qualified for playoffs via streak
2024 (5-12-1) was first missed playoffs since 2004 — losing record so no winningRecordNoPlayoff credit
All 5 playoff exits were in the West Semi-Final (Division Semifinal), mapping to Quarters (4pts each)
teams.notes: Calgary won 8 Grey Cups total (1948, 1971, 1992, 1998, 2001, 2008, 2014, 2018). Since the 2018 title, they have exited in the West Semi-Final (first playoff round) five times in six playoff appearances. In 2024 they missed the playoffs entirely with a losing record. The PPI score is 4*5 = 20.

flags: Saskatchewan won the 2025 Grey Cup on November 16, 2025, defeating Montreal 25-17. The drought window begins in 2026, which is the current in-progress season. No completed seasons exist in the drought window, so all playoff loss counts are 0.
teams.notes: Saskatchewan's previous championships were in 1966, 1989, 2007, and 2013. Their 2025 title is their fifth Grey Cup. The 2026 CFL season is currently in progress and does not count per the scoring rules.

flags: 2025 was the only completed season in the drought window; Argonauts went 5-13 and missed playoffs — no PPI events to count
teams.notes: Toronto won the 2024 Grey Cup (their 19th title). The drought window contains only the 2025 season, in which they finished 5-13 and were eliminated from playoff contention — a clearly losing record, so winningRecordNoPlayoff is 0. PPI score is 0.
